subject: math_aa_hl
syllabus: Mathematics: Analysis and Approaches (first assessment 2021), HL scope for May 2027
note: Student-facing KP framework paraphrased from the current IB course. All new KPs start covered=false until school coverage is confirmed.

kps.name: Arithmetic and geometric sequences and series; sigma notation
Financial applications: compound interest, depreciation and annuities
Exponents, logarithms and laws of indices
Binomial theorem, combinations and Pascal's triangle
Mathematical induction; direct and contradiction proofs
Complex numbers in Cartesian, polar and exponential form
Simultaneous linear equations and Gaussian elimination
Solutions of polynomial equations; roots, factors and conjugates
Error bounds, percentage error and approximation
Domain, range, inverse and composite functions
Graph transformations, symmetry and modulus
Quadratic, polynomial and rational functions; asymptotes
Exponential and logarithmic functions and equations
Growth and decay models; parameter interpretation
Solving equations graphically and numerically
Odd/even functions, self-inverse functions and restrictions
Polynomial roots, factor relationships and graph behaviour
Inequalities involving rational, exponential and logarithmic functions
Radians, arc length, sector area and unit-circle definitions
Trigonometric identities, equations and transformations
Sine rule, cosine rule and area of a triangle
Vectors, scalar product, angles and geometric applications
Vector equations of lines and planes in three dimensions
Intersections, distances and angles between lines and planes
Compound-angle, double-angle and half-angle identities
Inverse trigonometric functions and principal values
Complex numbers as rotations and roots of unity
Sampling, bias, reliability and presentation of data
Measures of central tendency, dispersion and outliers
Correlation, regression and interpretation of technology output
Set notation, sample spaces and conditional probability
Independent events, Bayes' theorem and tree diagrams
Discrete random variables and expected value
Binomial and normal distributions; inverse probability
Continuous random variables and probability density functions
Hypothesis testing: chi-squared and t-tests; Type I and II errors
Limits, continuity and derivative from first principles
Product, quotient and chain rules; implicit differentiation
Tangents, normals, optimization and related rates
Antiderivatives, definite integrals and area
Substitution, integration by parts and partial fractions
Volumes of revolution and improper integrals
First-order separable differential equations and models
Slope fields and Euler's method
Maclaurin series, convergence and approximation
